activities_projects.title: Observation of Onion Peel Cells
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure of plant cells
steps: Peel a thin layer from an onion bulb
Place it on a glass slide with a drop of water
Add safranin stain and cover with a cover slip
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies cell wall, nucleus, and cytoplasm in plant cells
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of diagram and observation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Cheek Cell Observation
steps: Gently scrape the inside of the cheek using a toothpick
Spread the sample on a glass slide
Add methylene blue stain
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of nucleus and cell membrane (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cell Model Making
steps: Prepare a 3D model of a plant or animal cell
Label organelles like nucleus, cytoplasm and membrane
Explain the function of each organelle

activities_projects.title: Diffusion Demonstration
steps: Fill a beaker with water
Add a drop of ink slowly
Observe how the ink spreads in water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains diffusion as movement from high to low concentration

activities_projects.title: Osmosis with Raisins
steps: Place raisins in plain water for a few hours
Place other raisins in salt water
Observe size changes in both samples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands movement of water across membranes

activities_projects.title: Cell Organelle Chart
steps: List organelles such as nucleus, mitochondria, ER, and Golgi apparatus
Draw simple diagrams
Write functions beside each organelle

activities_projects.title: Plant vs Animal Cell Comparison
steps: Draw diagrams of plant and animal cells
List differences between them
Highlight features like cell wall and vacuole

activities_projects.title: Microscope Parts Identification
steps: Observe a compound microscope
Identify parts like eyepiece, objective lens, and stage
Draw and label the microscope

activities_projects.title: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic Cells
steps: Write examples of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells
List major differences between them
Prepare a comparison table

activities_projects.title: Cell Organelles Matching Activity
steps: Write organelle names on cards
Write their functions on separate cards
Match each organelle with its function
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves memory of cell organelle functions
